sanskrit:
सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः।
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः॥
transliteration:
santuṣṭaḥ satataṃ yogī yatātmā dṛḍhaniścayaḥ |
mayy arpitamano-buddhir yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Ever-content, constantly united (yogī), self-controlled, firm in resolve; one whose mind and intellect are offered to Me—such a devotee is dear to Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the inner marks of the person who has moved from anxious self-protection to secure self-transcendence: stable contentment, disciplined attention, unwavering commitment, and full cognitive surrender to the sacred.
psychological_state: Secure-functioning adult: low reactivity, high distress tolerance, integrated identity, and an internal working model that locates safety in relationship with the transcendent rather than in external validation.
trigger_scenario: You’re stuck in traffic, late for an important meeting; instead of spiraling into road-rage or self-criticism you exhale, accept the delay, use the pause to mentally offer the moment to something larger, and arrive centered rather than frazzled.
anchor_text: Rest in steady contentment, offering mind and will to the sacred.
